title: Joseph and the Amazing Technicolor Dreamcoat
description: GEORGE NEWS - The colourful and timeless...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The colourful and timeless musical, Joseph and the Amazing Technicolor Dreamcoat, is set to entertain audiences at the George Arts Theatre starting on 26 June.

Featuring an exuberant cast of young, local talent from the Garden Route Area, this production promises a spectacular blend of music, dance, and storytelling.


> The production is running in the June school holidays, which makes it a perfect evening out for the entire family.


Show director Quintin van Biljon is known for his successful stints with Seussical the Musical and last year’s hit Grease. With this production of Joseph, Van Biljon brings a fresh vibrancy to the beloved show, ensuring that it resonates with audiences of all ages.

The show schedule is as follows: evening performances at 19:00 from 26 - 29 June and 3 - 6 July. Additionally, matinée shows will be available at 14:00 on 29 June and 6 July. Tickets are available at Computicket at R150 (adults), R120 (members and children).Follow the story of Joseph

Joseph and the Amazing Technicolor Dreamcoat is a vibrant musical that chronicles the life of Joseph, a dreamer blessed with the ability to interpret dreams. Favored by his father and envied by his brothers, Joseph's life is turned upside down when he is sold into slavery in Egypt.

Despite his hardships, Joseph rises from slavery to become the pharaoh's trusted advisor, ultimately facing a dramatic reunion with his family. The story, set to a variety of musical styles, highlights themes of forgiveness, resilience, and destiny.


> "This thrilling musical adventure brings to life the Biblical story of Joseph, the favoured son with prophetic dreams, who embarks on a journey to Egypt after being sold into slavery by his brothers," says Van Biljon.


"Audiences will be treated to a captivating mix of musical styles, ranging from country-western to calypso, bubble-gum pop to rock 'n' roll. The production features popular songs like 'Go, Go, Go Joseph' and 'Any Dream Will Do,' performed by a young, vibrant and brilliant cast. This timeless tale of Joseph offers surprising humour for adults and dazzling delights for children."

For information contact the George Arts theatre (044 873 3938), send an email to [email protected] or follow the Facebook Page.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
